solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int minPartitions(string n) {
        return *max_element(n.begin(), n.end()) - '0';
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The minimum number of deci-binary numbers needed to sum up to a given number is equal to the maximum digit in the number. This is because each deci-binary number can contribute at most 1 to each digit position. The solution efficiently finds the maximum digit in the input string.
